text: Foundation models (Bommasani et al. 2021, Stanford CRFM) are large-scale models trained on broad data that can be adapted to a wide range of downstream tasks via fine-tuning or prompting.

Chinchilla scaling laws (Hoffmann et al. 2022, DeepMind) showed that for compute-optimal training, model size and training tokens should scale equally — many LLMs were undertrained relative to their parameter count.
BERT (Devlin et al. 2019) pioneered the pretrain-then-finetune paradigm with masked language modeling, establishing the foundation model approach that led to GPT, T5, and modern LLMs.

GPT-4 (OpenAI 2023) demonstrated that scaling foundation models yields multimodal capabilities, advanced reasoning, and human-level performance on professional benchmarks like the bar exam (90th percentile).
